title: The Peacock's Complaint
content:
The Peacock considered it wrong
    That he had not the nightingale's song;
    So to Juno he went,
    She replied, "Be content
    With thy having, & hold thy fool's tongue!"

    Do Not Quarrel With Nature
meaning:
"The Peacock's Complaint" is a quintessential example of Walter Crane's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
